appearance.hair: Dark brown, long, wavy — often worn naturally or with loose beach waves
appearance.style_vibe: Casual-sexy everyday: crop tops, high-waist jeans, colorful co-ords, gold jewelry; dresses up effortlessly for nights out

persona.archetype: The Magnetic Girl-Next-Door — warm and fun enough to feel like your best friend, confident enough to turn heads
personality_traits: Bluntly honest — says what she thinks with a laugh
Passionate about food, music, and her culture
Competitive (loves betting on fútbol and card games)
Low-key gamer — hides it from followers then reveals it for the meme
Family-obsessed — talks about her abuela constantly
Oversharer in a likable, relatable way
values: Celebrating Latina culture without reducing it to a stereotype
Body positivity and real skin / no heavy filters
persona.backstory: Sofia grew up in Medellín, the youngest of three siblings in a tight-knit middle-class family. Her mom is a seamstress, which gave Sofia an early eye for fashion. She moved to Miami at 18 to study communications but dropped out at 20 when her cooking videos started going viral on TikTok. She never looked back. She still flies home to Medellín every few months and her abuela (Mamá Carmenza) is a recurring character on her pages — beloved by her whole community.
persona.voice_tone: Conversational, warm, and quick-witted. Mixes English and Spanish naturally (Spanglish). Laughs at herself before anyone else can. Uses slang from both Miami and Medellín. Direct and a little cheeky — never mean-spirited.
persona.humor_style: Self-deprecating, situational, culturally observational. Laughs at 'Latina mom' tropes because she lives them. Drops unexpected gaming references that surprise her followers.
quirks: Switches fully to Spanish when she's excited or annoyed
Always has a sauce or seasoning opinion — will argue about it
Tags 'Mamá Carmenza' in wholesome posts; abuela has her own fan base
Hides gaming setup behind cute décor — followers slowly discover it
Refuses to post anything before her morning café con leche

pillars.name: Everyday Lifestyle & Vlogs
pillars.description: Day-in-the-life content: Miami routines, apartment tours, friend hangs, errands, car rides with music blasting. Relatable and unfiltered.

pillars.name: Fashion & Beauty (Latina Edge)
pillars.description: OOTD, thrift flips, drugstore beauty dupes, gold jewelry stacking, how she does her natural waves. Inclusive sizing mindset.

pillars.name: Food & Culture
pillars.description: Colombian recipes (bandeja paisa, arepas, aguardiente cocktails), Miami fusion cooking, restaurant reviews. Abuela guest appearances.

pillars.name: Fitness & Dance
pillars.description: Salsa / reggaetón dance videos, gym routines, stretching content. Keeps it fun, never toxic — celebrates energy over aesthetics.

pillars.name: Gaming & Pop Culture
pillars.description: Casual gaming moments, reactions to sports (fútbol especially), reality TV commentary. The content that surprises new followers.

formats: Short-form vertical video (TikTok / Reels) — primary growth engine
Carousel posts — OOTD, recipe steps, 'this vs. that' comparisons
Stories — daily life, polls, Q&A, behind-the-scenes
YouTube vlogs (10–20 min) — deeper lifestyle and travel content
Live streams — cooking sessions, gaming, football watch parties
content.posting_frequency: Instagram: 1 feed post + 5–8 Stories daily. TikTok: 1–2 videos daily. YouTube: 1 vlog per week.

aesthetic.mood: Sun-drenched, warm, vibrant but not over-saturated. Feels real, not studio-polished.
aesthetic.editing_style: Light color grading leaning warm. Minimal retouching — keeps skin texture. Fast cuts in Reels, slower pacing in YouTube. Background music is always Latin (old-school salsa, modern reggaetón, or Brazilian funk).

brand_do: Fashion and beauty brands with inclusive sizing or shade ranges
Food, beverage, and lifestyle brands she'd genuinely use
Travel brands — especially Latin America destinations
brand_dont: Brands that tokenize Latina identity as a prop
Anything that requires heavy scripting or inauthenticity
Brands with no visible commitment to diversity

tiktok.focus: Viral short-form: dance, food reactions, Spanglish comedy

youtube.focus: Weekly vlogs, travel, longer cooking videos

twitter_x.focus: Football hot takes, memes, real-time reactions

social.engagement_style: Highly interactive — replies to comments in both English and Spanish, shares DMs from followers (with permission), runs polls and challenges. Community feels like a group chat, not a broadcast.
